findings.reachability: Any caller that passes a user-controlled IPv6 string to the exported default function — including SSRF-protection middleware relying on this library to block private IPs.
findings.justification: Line 61 calls ipv6_check(ip) with the raw, un-normalized input string; the regexes on lines 43-50 are case-sensitive (no /i flag), so inputs such as ::FFFF:127.0.0.1, 2001:DB8::1, or 64:FF9B::127.0.0.1 are validated as legitimate IPv6 by ipaddr.js but pass through ipv6_check undetected, causing the function to return false/undefined instead of true.

detection_pattern.section_name: SSRF filter bypass: un-normalized IPv6 passed to case-sensitive regex
detection_pattern.description: Detects places where an IPv6 string is routed to a hand-written case-sensitive regex check using the raw (un-normalized) input rather than a normalized lowercase form, enabling private-range bypass via uppercase characters.

findings.reachability: Any caller that treats a falsy return (undefined) as 'not private' — the most common defensive pattern.
findings.justification: When ipaddr.js validates the input as IPv6 but none of the handwritten regexes match (e.g. due to the case-bypass above), the function falls through ipv6_check() returning false, and then the outer function itself returns undefined — callers using if (!privateIp(addr)) allow() silently permit the request.

detection_pattern.section_name: Insecure design: undefined as 'not private' sentinel
detection_pattern.description: Flags IP-classification helpers that return undefined rather than an explicit boolean, which causes callers relying on truthiness/falsiness to silently pass unknown or malformed addresses as non-private.
